steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts traditional sonnet cycles (Spenser's Amoretti, which follows a romantic-pursuit pattern) with McKay's "Cities," which uses the same form for an autobiographical travel narrative. The question asks what conclusion the passage most strongly supports.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — sonnet cycles are strongly associated with one subject, yet the form allows unorthodox exploration: matches the passage exactly. Spenser establishes the romantic tradition; McKay "subverts entrenched sonnet conventions" by using the form for travel narrative. ✓
  • Choice B — about differing perspectives: the passage never discusses varying "perspectives" on sonnet cycles — it discusses subject matter, not point of view. ✗
  • Choice C — about cohesiveness: the passage does not evaluate whether sonnet cycles are cohesive or not. ✗
  • Choice D — implies Spenser's Amoretti is novel: the passage presents Amoretti as the established tradition, not as something innovative. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
